objective: Use the K-41 development yard to test movement, sightlines, physics, helper tooling, AI lanes, structural primitives, and clipper groundwork inside a Source-style prototype map.
storyIntro: K-41 DEVELOPMENT YARD ACTIVE. THIS SANDBOX IS A FULL SOURCE-STYLE TEST MAP FOR BLOCKOUT READABILITY, TRAVERSAL, NPC SCALE, PHYSICS, LOGIC, AND STRUCTURAL PRIMITIVE AUTHORING.
guidanceHint: Start in the observation booth, sweep the central yard, test the west and east chambers, then climb into the north annex for pickups, logic, helper panels, and structural primitive demos.

geometry.text:
PLAYER SPAWNS SOUTH
PRIMITIVE BAY NORTH
TRICKERY ROW\nFAKE FILLET  HULL FIX  CHEAP CONVEX PHYSICS  ADAPTIVE DETAIL  ORGANIC MELT  RECURSIVE DEPTH
UTILITY ROW\nDAMAGE  KILL  CAMERA  SAFE ZONE  PROBES  FOG  CULLING  GRAVITY  WIND  BUOYANCY
MISSING-BATCH ROW\nEXTRUDE  CABLE  AI BLOCKER  STREAM PRELOAD  CHECKPOINT  GI BOUNDS  FOG BLOCKER  PIVOT
SOURCE-STYLE GAP ROW\nSUPERELLIPSOID  PROFILE  PIPE RAMPS  WATER  NAV HOLE  LADDER  CAMERA CLIP  RADIAL FORCE  PHYSICS CONSTRAINT  AUDIO OCCLUSION  SHADOW EXCLUSION  LIGHT PORTAL
WORLD-OPS ROW\nTERRAIN CUTOUT  HINT/SKIP  NAV COST  GENERIC TRIGGER  TELEPORT  LAUNCH  AMBIENT AUDIO  LOD OVERRIDE

spawners.name: Patrol Subject
spawners.dialogue: PATROL SUBJECT: placeholder mesh until Raw Iron ships character art. Use for scale and lane layout.

spawners.title: Development Yard
spawners.interactText: Development yard overview: this test level is rebuilt to read like a Source-engine prototype facility. Use the central yard for movement and sightline checks, the west wing for friendly NPC patrol reads, the east wing for physics and helper validation, and the north annex for pickups, logic, and structural primitives.

spawners.title: Physics Chamber
spawners.interactText: Physics chamber: kick the wooden block crate for heavy-object feel, kick the glow ball for rolling response, and hold Shift plus E on the casualty bundle to lift it. Left click throws the body.

spawners.title: West Lane
lines: Full character AI ships in Raw Iron
This shell is display + input only
spawners.interactText: West lane: use the friendly NPC path to validate scale, turn-in-place feel, and simple patrol presentation. Opponent, encounter, and encounter-combat logic belong in native Raw Iron titles and tools—not in this lightweight web preview.

spawners.title: East Lane
spawners.interactText: East lane: physics props and helper exercises only. Implement pursuit, melee commit, and hit resolution in your Raw Iron game module or editor preview—this prototype intentionally avoids opponent entities.

spawners.title: Pickup Bay
spawners.interactText: Pickup bay annex: use this volume to judge billboard/readability and lighting balance (inventory pickups are not simulated in this JS prototype).

spawners.title: Rear gallery
spawners.interactText: K-41 DEVELOPMENT YARD: use this room to judge silhouette, trim language, light rhythm, and room-to-room scale before touching campaign spaces.

spawners.title: Rear gallery
spawners.interactText: K-41 DEVELOPMENT YARD: the wings are split by purpose. West is patrol routes and cover reads. East is physics and helpers. North is pickups, logic, and structural primitive authoring.

spawners.title: Structural Primitive Bay
spawners.interactText: Structural primitive bay: this annex now exercises the remaining prototype-side terrain, helper, operator, and Source-style brush-entity equivalents. The latest curved-profile donor batch has been removed from protoengine and promoted to RawIron native structural compilation.

spawners.title: Convex Clipper
spawners.interactText: Convex clipper foundation: the engine now has its own plane-based convex solid splitter plus compile-time boolean union, intersection, difference, and subtractive cutter support for additive structural solids, not just the original box room shells. It also carries visibility-plane metadata, clipping volumes, spatial query volumes, toggleable occlusion portals, tri-planar texture overrides, bevel tagging, and decal projectors for structural overlays.

spawners.title: Transition Operators
spawners.interactText: Transition operators: this protoengine donor row has been pared down after RawIron absorbed the promoted curved-profile and lofting families as native C++ structural primitives. The remaining prototype hall keeps only enough transition scaffolding alive for future harvesting.

spawners.title: Source Runtime Row
spawners.interactText: Source runtime row: the back operator bay now maps to classic Source-style brush behavior too. Filtered collision volumes act like targeted clip brushes, pass-through primitives read like illusionary solids, translation and rotation primitives cover sliding doors and rotating blockers, spline path followers cover moving path geometry, surface velocity primitives act like conveyors, traversal links act like ladders, fluid simulation volumes combine buoyancy and underwater FX, volumetric emitter bounds confine fog/rain-style particles, render-target and planar-reflection primitives cover screens and mirrors, structural-detail modifiers mark colliders as non-structural shell detail, and sky projection surfaces give a lightweight sky-window surface.

spawners.title: Helper Library Bay
lines: Use dev tools / native build for scripted chains
spawners.interactText: Helper library bay: this support alcove proves the layer is live inside the engine. Crossing volumes still raises trigger hooks on the runtime bus; authored entity input/output graphs are intentionally omitted from this JS prototype.

spawners.title: Bus Ping Feed
spawners.interactText: Bus ping feed: entering the helper bay still drives trigger hooks for the runtime bus and helper metrics. This preview no longer runs authored entity wiring graphs from level JSON.

spawners.title: Logic (Raw Iron)
spawners.interactText: ProtoEngine no longer runs logic_relay, logic_timer, logic_counter, or logic_compare spawners. The authoring model and example graphs live in docs/LOGIC_ENTITIES_ENGINE_AGNOSTIC_SPEC.md for the production engine.

spawners.title: Engine Status
spawners.interactText: Engine status: this panel reads live runtime counts from the sandbox so the room doubles as a quick systems-health check.

spawners.title: Bus Ping
spawners.interactText: Interact labels remain for UX reads; scripted entity chains are compiled for the native engine instead of this JS sandbox.

spawners.title: Portal Toggle
spawners.interactText: Demonstrates occlusion portal placement; runtime toggle wiring belongs in the native graph/behavior layer rather than this prototype.
